forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred over eastern NSW will allow a cold front to move across the far southeast of SA today. A second high pressure system is expected to move south of the Bight during Friday and Saturday ahead of another trough expected to brush the west and south on the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Variable about 10 knots becoming southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the middle of the day. Winds reaching up to 20 knots offshore in the evening.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore by early evening.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Sunny.
Southeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning easterly 15 to 20 knots early in the morning.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 2 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy.
Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots shifting south to southwesterly during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon or evening.
Sunny morning. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: Variable about 10 knots becoming southeast to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the early afternoon then tending southeasterly in the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Sunny.
Southeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning easterly in the evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Mostly sunny.
East to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots tending north to northeasterly during the morning then becoming variable about 10 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Mostly sunny day. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.text: Variable below 10 knots becoming south to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Sunny.
Southeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning easterly in the evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
East to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly 10 to 15 knots during the day.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Mostly sunny day. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
West to northwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning south to southwesterly below 10 knots in the middle of the day.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning easterly 15 to 20 knots in the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
East to northeasterly 15 to 20 knots turning northerly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the evening.
Mostly sunny day. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
